title: Nephrodium pica
taxonomy: Nephrodium pica is classified within the division Pteridophyta, which includes ferns and their allies. It belongs to the class Polypodiopsida and further classified into the order Polypodiales. This plant is grouped into the family Dryopteridaceae, a family of ferns commonly known as the wood fern family. The genus Nephrodium is now considered a synonym of Dryopteris, suggesting that Nephrodium pica is now classified as Dryopteris pica within the family Dryopteridaceae. This fern is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world.
uses: Nephrodium pica, also known as the Lady fern, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating skin conditions and wounds. In some cultures, it is also used to treat fever and digestive issues. However, more research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Nephrodium pica for medicinal purposes. It is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any plant for medicinal treatment.
